title: Remediate pre-existing Slither High/Medium findings baselined in HOK-1734
description:
## Background

[HOK-1734](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-1734/support-slither-static-analysis) introduced the Slither static analysis gate. During the initial scan, the following pre-existing High and Medium severity findings were discovered. They were baselined (accepted into `slither-baseline.json`) with justification that contract remediation was outside [HOK-1734](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-1734/support-slither-static-analysis) scope.

This issue tracks the remediation of those findings.

## Findings to Remediate

### High Severity

| Detector | Count | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `arbitrary-send-eth` | 3 | Functions that send ETH to an arbitrary caller-controlled address |
| `reentrancy-eth` | 4 | Functions vulnerable to reentrancy attacks involving ETH transfers |
| `unchecked-transfer` | 1 | ERC20 `transfer`/`transferFrom` return value not checked |

### Medium Severity

| Detector | Count | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `divide-before-multiply` | 1 | Integer division before multiplication may truncate result |
| `incorrect-equality` | 3 | Dangerous use of strict equality (`==`) on balances or token amounts |

## Approach

For each finding:

1. Run `npm run slither:report` to see the exact location of each finding.
2. Assess whether the finding is a false positive or a real vulnerability.
3. If real: fix the contract logic.
4. If false positive: document the justification clearly in `slither-baseline.json` and update the `followUp` field to reference this issue.

Once all genuine findings are fixed or confirmed as false positives, remove the corresponding entries from `slither-baseline.json` so the gate enforces them going forward.

## References

* [HOK-1734](https://linear.app/hokusai/issue/HOK-1734/support-slither-static-analysis) (adds the Slither gate, baselined these findings)
* `slither-baseline.json` — entries with these check names
* `docs/slither-static-analysis.md` — runbook for baseline workflow
